summary: Frontend HUD redesign verified live (Expo web + claude-3-5-haiku) over a fresh 3-turn Horror/hard chronicle. ALL six product requirements PASS: (1) NO 'objective-bar' testID anywhere across 3 turns; (2) DNG chip (testID 'state-danger') rendered every turn with vocabulary {elevated, high, elevated} - all in allowed set {none,low,elevated,high,critical}; (3) MOM chip (testID 'state-momentum') rendered every turn with vocabulary {stalling, declining, steady} - all in allowed set {surging,steady,stalling,declining,lost}; (4) PRS line (testID 'pressure-bar') present every turn with non-prescriptive condition phrases: 'Unknown movement in the darkness' / 'Fresh leg wound bleeding' / 'Leg wound requires continued care' - none match the prescriptive regex (find/locate/get/reach/escape/use/talk/kill/must/should/objective/...); (5) NO relationship vector leak (trust/loyalty/fear/resentment substrings absent from rendered DOM on all 3 turns); (6) Regression clean - HP/STR/FTG still rendered, choices A/B/C tappable, custom action input works, paragraphs/narrative render via Animated.Text, no crash, no red screen, only one harmless RN-web deprecation warning ('props.pointerEvents is deprecated'). The 'Leg wound requires continued care' phrase is borderline (the word 'requires' implies action is wanted) but it does NOT contain quest-marker language and is not flagged by the engine's _PRESCRIPTIVE_RE - recording as a soft observation, not a bug.

issues: Top status bar uses statRow with flexWrap and 5 chips (HP/STR/FTG/DNG/MOM) on a 390px viewport - chips wrap to two rows once all five are present (observed in turn-1 screenshot HP/STR/FTG row 1, DNG/MOM row 2). Spacing is fine and readable on mobile; not a bug, but main agent may want to compact chip width or relabel to keep all 5 on one row in landscape / on wider phones.

PRS line 'Leg wound requires continued care' (turn 3) is borderline prescriptive - 'requires' nudges toward instruction language. Not caught by hud.py _PRESCRIPTIVE_RE and not a regression, but worth considering adding 'requires|need(s)?' to the regex to harden non-prescriptive guarantee.

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'. Origin is inside react-native / reanimated runtime (not user code in play/[id].tsx). Harmless on web preview but should be tracked. Reference: console output captured during play screen render.

action_items: OPTIONAL hardening: extend hud.py _PRESCRIPTIVE_RE to also reject 'requires|needs' so phrases like 'wound requires continued care' fall back to the engine-derived 'Wound throbbing' phrasing.
OPTIONAL: track the 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated' RN-web warning - investigate which dependency (likely react-native or reanimated) emits it and bump if a non-deprecated version exists.
critical_code_review_comments: frontend/app/play/[id].tsx correctly removes the OBJ bar: there is NO 'objective-bar' testID in the file, only 'pressure-bar' (line 257). chip() helper at line 58 cleanly returns null when the engine omits a key, so Pressure correctly disappears when state has no Pressure key (line 256: `{pressure ? ... : null}`).
DANGER_COLOR_MAP and MOMENTUM_COLOR_MAP (lines 42-56) cover the full vocab set from hud.py (DANGER_VALUES, MOMENTUM_VALUES). Color choices are sensible (severity-graded).

Pressure rendering uses numberOfLines={1} (line 259). For phrases like 'Leg wound requires continued care' this could truncate on narrow devices; consider numberOfLines={2} or remove the limit since the bar already has its own line.

Relationship vectors (trust/loyalty/fear/resentment) are NOT referenced anywhere in play/[id].tsx player UI - they would only surface in the dev rolling_state debug dump (line 327), which is gated by `devUnlocked && debugMode`. Correctly hidden by default.

backend/hud.py _PRESCRIPTIVE_RE is well-tuned: catches verbs (find/locate/reach/get/...) and modal duty words (must/should/have to). Could be hardened with 'require(s|d)?' to catch the live LLM phrasing observed in this run.

success_rate.frontend: 6/6 HUD requirements pass live (objective removed, DNG chip valid vocab, MOM chip valid vocab, PRS present every turn, PRS non-prescriptive, relationship vectors hidden, regression clean). 3/3 turns rendered in ~45-55s each under claude-3-5-haiku.

seed_data_creation: Ephemeral chronicle (Horror, hard) created via UI; not cleaned up - main agent may delete via UI menu or DB if persistent.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Live LLM phrasing observed for PRS over 3 horror turns: 'Unknown movement in the darkness', 'Fresh leg wound bleeding', 'Leg wound requires continued care'. None tripped the regex but the third uses 'requires' which is semantically close to 'must/should/need'. If future iterations tighten the rule, also tighten _PRESCRIPTIVE_RE in /app/backend/hud.py. To reproduce: open https://dice-engine-3.preview.emergentagent.com → New Story → genre 'horror' → role 'lone scavenger' → difficulty 'hard' → Roll For Initiative. Wait ~45s per turn. To stress PRS, type custom actions like 'force the rusted door and gash my leg'. testIDs in play/[id].tsx: state-danger, state-momentum, pressure-bar, choice-A/B/C/D, custom-action-input, send-action-btn.
rca_of_issue: No issue. HUD redesign verified end-to-end. The OBJ bar is fully removed, DNG/MOM chips render with correct vocabulary, PRS is grounded and non-prescriptive, and dev-only relationship vectors stay hidden in the player UI as designed.
